title: Acute Annular Outer Retinopathy
description: AAOR is a rare disorder classically characterised by a peripapillary annular band of gray-white opacification of the deep retina. On OCT it is characterised by thinning or atrophy of the outer retina/ONL, including disruption of the EZ within the annular area of RPE destruction.

title: Acute Macular Neuroretinopathy (AMN)
description: AMN is a rare outer retinopathy that often affects young women after a viral prodrome and causes paracentral scotomas. On OCT it is characterised by acute hyper-reflectivity in the outer nuclear or outer plexiform region followed by focal ellipsoid zone disruption.

title: Acute Posterior Multifocal Placoid Pigment Epitheliopathy (APMPPE)
description: APMPPE is an acquired inflammatory uveitis and an uncommon White Dot Syndrome. On OCT it is characterised by hyper-reflectivity from the OPL to the RPE with normal retinal thickness. Some patients may have subretinal fluid or intraretinal cysts.

title: Acute Retinal Pigment Epitheliitis (Krill Disease)
description: ARPE is an idiopathic retinopathy typically occurring in young adults (ages 10-40). On OCT it is characterised by is a dome-shaped hyper-reflective lesion at the photoreceptor outer segment layer disrupting the EZ and IZ.

title: Acute Syphilitic Placoid Chorioretinitis
description: Acute syphilitic placoid chorioretinitis is an infectious chorioretinitis caused by ocular syphilis. On OCT it is characterised by ellipsoid zone loss, granular RPE irregularity or excrescences, and placoid subretinal hyper-reflective change at the posterior pole.

title: Acute Zonal Occult Outer Retinopathy (AZOOR)
description: AZOOR is a rare outer retinopathy that typically presents with photopsias and visual field loss in young to middle-aged women. On OCT it is characterised by focal outer retinal and ellipsoid zone disruption, often corresponding to a trizonal pattern on fundus autofluorescence.

title: Asteroid Hyalosis
description: AH is a common degenerative condition characterised by calcium-lipid complexes in the vitreous. On OCT it is characterised by hyper-reflective dots in the vitreous with a shadowing effect on the retina.

title: Astrocytic Hamartoma
description: Astrocytic hamartoma is a benign glial retinal or optic nerve lesion often associated with tuberous sclerosis complex. On OCT it is characterised by an elevated hyper-reflective mass with a nodular moth-eaten appearance from intralesional optically empty spaces or calcification.

title: Bacillary Detachment
description: Bacillary detachment refers to splitting within the photoreceptor layer, typically at the myoid zone just posterior to the external limiting membrane. On OCT it is characterised by a cystic cavity between the ELM and ellipsoid zone, often with associated serous retinal detachment.

title: Best Vitelliform Macular Dystrophy
description: BVMD is a genetic maculopathy caused by a mutation in the BEST1 gene. On OCT it is characterised by pyramid-shaped hyper-reflective material in the sub-retinal space corresponding to the egg yolk-like macular lesions on fundus photographs.

title: Bilateral optic neuritis related to multiple sclerosis (MS)
description: Optic neuritis related to multiple sclerosis is an inflammatory optic neuropathy that commonly presents with pain on eye movement and reduced colour vision. OCT may show acute RNFL swelling early on, followed by thinning of the RNFL and ganglion cell complex after the episode resolves.

title: Birdshot Chorioretinopathy
description: Birdshot chorioretinopathy is a bilateral white-dot syndrome with characteristic cream-coloured choroidal lesions. On OCT it is characterised by parafoveal ellipsoid zone loss and may be accompanied by cystoid macular edema, epiretinal membrane, or vitreous inflammation.

title: Bitemporal Hemianopia
description: Bitemporal hemianopia localizes to chiasmal dysfunction, most often from compression. On OCT it is characterised by binasal ganglion cell complex thinning that mirrors temporal visual field loss.

title: Blood Vessels
description: Retinal blood vessels appear as focal inner retinal hyper-reflective profiles on OCT. They commonly cast vertical posterior shadowing into the deeper retinal layers.

title: BRAF/MEK Inhibitor-Induced Papillitis
description: BRAF or MEK inhibitor induced papillitis is a treatment-related inflammatory optic disc edema seen in patients receiving targeted melanoma therapy. On OCT it is characterised by RNFL thickening and optic nerve head swelling, often in an asymmetric bilateral pattern.

title: Branch Retinal Artery Occlusion (BRAO)
description: BRAO is a retinal vascular occlusion that causes sectoral inner retinal ischemia and corresponding field loss. On OCT it is characterised acutely by inner retinal hyper-reflectivity and swelling, and chronically by focal inner retinal thinning and atrophy.

title: Cancer Associated Retinopathy
description: Cancer-associated retinopathy is a paraneoplastic autoimmune retinopathy that produces progressive photoreceptor dysfunction. On OCT it is characterised by diffuse outer retinal attenuation with loss of the ellipsoid zone and thinning of the photoreceptor layers.

title: Cavernous Hemangioma
description: Orbital cavernous hemangioma is a benign intraconal vascular lesion that can indent the globe and distort the posterior pole. On OCT it may produce choroidal or retinal folds, optic nerve head elevation, and secondary peripapillary change from mass effect.

title: Central Areolar Choroidal Dystrophy (CACD)
description: CACD is an inherited macular dystrophy with progressive atrophy of the retinal pigment epithelium, choriocapillaris, and outer retina. On OCT it is characterised by central outer retinal and RPE loss with increased choroidal transmission.

title: Central Retinal Artery Occlusion (CRAO)
description: CRAO is an ophthalmic emergency caused by acute retinal arterial ischemia. On OCT it is characterised by diffuse inner retinal hyper-reflectivity and thickening acutely, followed by marked inner retinal thinning and atrophy chronically.

title: Central Serous Chorioretinopathy (CSCR)
description: CSCR is a pachychoroid-spectrum disorder caused by serous detachment of the neurosensory retina. On OCT it is characterised by subretinal fluid, a thickened choroid with pachyvessels, and sometimes small pigment epithelial detachments or a double-layer sign.

title: Choroidal Atrophy
description: Choroidal atrophy refers to focal or diffuse loss of choroidal tissue with secondary overlying outer retinal degeneration. On OCT it is characterised by marked choroidal thinning with attenuation of the RPE and photoreceptor layers.

title: Choroidal Excavation
description: Focal choroidal excavation is a localized concavity of the choroid detected on OCT. It may be conforming or non-conforming, with the latter showing separation between photoreceptors and the RPE.

title: Choroidal Excavation with Subretinal Fluid
description: Choroidal excavation with subretinal fluid is a focal choroidal concavity complicated by overlying neurosensory detachment. On OCT it is characterised by choroidal depression with adjacent or overlying subretinal fluid and outer retinal disruption.

title: Choroidal Hemangioma
description: Choroidal hemangioma is a benign vascular choroidal tumour that can leak and distort the overlying retina. On OCT it is characterised by an elevated choroidal mass with overlying subretinal fluid, outer retinal change, and sometimes cystoid edema.

title: Choroidal Hyporeflective Caverns
description: Choroidal hyporeflective caverns are optically empty spaces seen within the choroid in degenerative or pachychoroid conditions. On OCT they appear as angular or round hyporeflective cavities without a hyper-reflective wall, often with posterior hypertransmission.

title: Choroidal Metastasis
description: Choroidal metastasis is the most common intraocular metastatic tumour and often arises from breast or lung cancer. On OCT it is characterised by an undulating choroidal mass with overlying subretinal fluid, shaggy photoreceptors, and RPE irregularity.

title: Choroidal Metastatic Lung Adenocarcinoma
description: Choroidal metastatic lung adenocarcinoma presents as a metastatic choroidal mass from systemic lung cancer. On OCT it is characterised by a choroidal elevation with overlying subretinal fluid, outer retinal disruption, and RPE undulation.

title: Choroidal Neovascular Membrane
description: Choroidal neovascular membrane is pathologic new vessel growth through Bruch membrane into the sub-RPE or subretinal space. On OCT it is characterised by subretinal or sub-RPE hyper-reflective material with associated fluid, hemorrhage, or pigment epithelial detachment.

title: Choroidal Neovascular Membrane (Type 3)
description: Type 3 neovascularization, or retinal angiomatous proliferation, arises from the deep retina and extends toward the RPE and choroid. On OCT it is characterised by intraretinal hyper-reflective foci, cystic edema, pigment epithelial detachment, and associated subretinal or intraretinal fluid.

title: Choroidal Nevus
description: Choroidal nevus is a benign melanocytic choroidal lesion that is usually flat or minimally elevated. On OCT it may show smooth choroidal thickening with overlying drusen, RPE change, or shallow subretinal fluid when suspicious.

title: Choroidal Nevus with Choroidal Neovascularization
description: Choroidal nevus with secondary neovascularization is a nevus complicated by growth of a CNVM. On OCT it is characterised by a choroidal nevus with overlying subretinal hyper-reflective material, subretinal fluid, and outer retinal disturbance.

title: Choroidal Rupture
description: Choroidal rupture is a break in Bruch membrane, the RPE, and choriocapillaris, most often after blunt trauma. On OCT it is characterised by discontinuity and deformation of the RPE-Bruch complex with adjacent outer retinal disruption and sometimes secondary CNVM.

title: Chronic Branch Retinal Artery Occlusion
description: Chronic BRAO is the late atrophic stage of sectoral retinal arterial ischemia. On OCT it is characterised by focal inner retinal thinning, loss of normal lamination, and chronic atrophy in the affected vascular territory.

title: Cilioretinal artery occlusion
description: Cilioretinal artery occlusion causes localized inner retinal ischemia in the distribution of the cilioretinal artery. On OCT it is characterised by parafoveal inner retinal hyper-reflectivity acutely and subsequent focal inner retinal thinning.

title: CNVM associated with Angioid Streaks
description: Angioid streaks can predispose to breaks in Bruch membrane and secondary choroidal neovascularization. On OCT it is characterised by subretinal or sub-RPE hyper-reflective material with associated fluid and disruption of the outer retina overlying the streak.

title: Combined Hamartoma of Retina and Retinal Pigment Epithelium (CHRRPE)
description: CHRRPE is a benign congenital lesion involving disorganized retina, RPE, and epiretinal gliosis. On OCT it is characterised by marked retinal thickening, architectural distortion, and tractional change from an overlying epiretinal membrane.

title: Compressive Optic Neuropathy
description: Compressive optic neuropathy is optic nerve dysfunction caused by orbital, sellar, or intracranial mass effect. On OCT it is characterised by thinning of the RNFL and ganglion cell complex, sometimes in band or sectoral patterns depending on the site of compression.

title: Cone Dystrophy
description: Cone dystrophy is an inherited retinal degeneration that primarily affects cone photoreceptors and central vision. On OCT it is characterised by foveal outer retinal thinning with disruption or loss of the ellipsoid zone.

title: Cystic Retinal Tuft
description: Cystic retinal tuft is a peripheral vitreoretinal anomaly associated with focal retinal thinning and traction. On OCT it is characterised by a sharply elevated lesion with cystic change and adjacent vitreoretinal adhesion.

title: Cystoid Macular Edema
description: Cystoid macular edema is accumulation of fluid within the neurosensory retina in a cystic pattern. On OCT it is characterised by hyporeflective intraretinal cystic spaces, often in the inner nuclear and outer plexiform layers, with increased macular thickness.

title: Cytomegalovirus (CMV) Retinitis
description: CMV retinitis is a necrotizing infectious retinitis seen most often in immunocompromised patients. On OCT it may show full-thickness retinal hyper-reflectivity, retinal disorganization, edema, and subsequent thinning in areas of necrosis.

title: Dark-without-pressure
description: Dark-without-pressure is a peripheral retinal phenomenon that can resemble retinal pathology clinically but is typically benign. On OCT it has been associated with abrupt changes in ellipsoid zone reflectivity and subtle outer retinal contour alteration.

title: Diabetic Macular Edema (DME)
description: DME is vision-threatening retinal thickening caused by vascular leakage in diabetes. On OCT it is characterised by intraretinal cysts, diffuse retinal thickening, and sometimes subretinal fluid or hyper-reflective foci.

title: Diffuse Unilateral Subacute Neuroretinitis (DUSN)
description: DUSN is an inflammatory retinopathy caused by subretinal nematode infection. On OCT it may show focal outer retinal disruption early and later diffuse retinal thinning with optic nerve and RNFL loss.

title: Disorganization of the Retinal Inner Layers (DRIL)
description: DRIL describes loss of clear boundaries between the ganglion cell-inner plexiform complex, inner nuclear layer, and outer plexiform layer. On OCT it appears as confluent inner retinal disorganization and is associated with poorer macular visual function.

title: Dominant Optic Atrophy (DOA)
description: Dominant optic atrophy is an inherited optic neuropathy, most commonly related to OPA1 mutations. On OCT it is characterised by temporal RNFL loss and ganglion cell thinning, often with papillomacular bundle involvement.

title: Drusen
description: Drusen are extracellular deposits between the RPE and Bruch membrane, commonly seen in age-related macular degeneration. On OCT they appear as focal elevations of the RPE with variable internal reflectivity and contour.

title: Epiretinal Membrane (ERM)
description: Epiretinal membrane is a fibrocellular sheet on the inner retinal surface that can contract and distort the macula. On OCT it is characterised by a hyper-reflective membrane along the ILM with retinal wrinkling, thickening, and loss of the foveal contour.

title: Exudative Age-Related Macular Degeneration (nAMD)
description: Exudative AMD is neovascular age-related macular degeneration with leakage from choroidal neovascularization. On OCT it is characterised by subretinal or intraretinal fluid, pigment epithelial detachment, and subretinal hyper-reflective material.

title: Foveal Hypoplasia
description: Foveal hypoplasia is incomplete development of the foveal pit, often associated with albinism or other congenital eye conditions. On OCT it is characterised by absent foveal excavation and persistence of inner retinal layers at the foveal centre.

title: Full Thickness Macular Hole
description: Full-thickness macular hole is a complete defect through the neurosensory retina at the fovea, typically from vitreomacular traction. On OCT it is characterised by a full-thickness foveal opening with elevated edges, intraretinal cysts, and sometimes operculum or vitreous adherence.

title: Full Thickness Macular Hole from Epiretinal Membrane
description: Traction from an epiretinal membrane can precipitate a full-thickness macular hole. On OCT it is characterised by a full-thickness foveal defect with surrounding cystic change and persistent tangential traction from the ERM.

title: Fungal Retinitis
description: Fungal retinitis is an infectious chorioretinal process that may occur in immunocompromised or systemically septic patients. On OCT it may show focal hyper-reflective retinal infiltrates, vitreous cells, retinal edema, and disruption of adjacent outer retinal layers.

title: Geographic Atrophy
description: Geographic atrophy is the advanced atrophic form of AMD. On OCT it is characterised by sharply demarcated loss of the RPE, photoreceptors, and outer retina with marked choroidal hypertransmission.

title: Glaucoma
description: Glaucoma is a progressive optic neuropathy associated with characteristic loss of retinal ganglion cells and nerve fibers. On OCT it is characterised by thinning of the RNFL and ganglion cell complex, most often in the superior and inferior vulnerability zones.

title: Green Disease Glaucoma
description: Green disease refers to true glaucomatous damage that is missed by the device normative colour coding. On OCT it is characterised by suspicious inter-eye asymmetry or focal RNFL and GCC loss despite parameters remaining within the normal green range.

title: Hard Exudate
description: Hard exudates are lipid-rich deposits from chronic vascular leakage. On OCT they appear as small hyper-reflective intraretinal foci, often with posterior shadowing, commonly adjacent to edema.

title: Homonymous Thinning
description: Homonymous thinning reflects retrograde degeneration of retinal ganglion cells after post-chiasmal visual pathway injury. On OCT it is characterised by hemimacular ganglion cell thinning that respects the vertical meridian and mirrors the homonymous visual field defect.

title: Hydroxychloroquine (Plaquenil) Toxicity
description: Hydroxychloroquine toxicity is a dose and duration dependent maculopathy. On OCT it is characterised by parafoveal or pericentral outer retinal and ellipsoid zone loss with relative foveal preservation in early disease.

title: Hypotony Maculopathy
description: Hypotony maculopathy is structural macular change caused by abnormally low intraocular pressure, often after filtration surgery. On OCT it is characterised by chorioretinal folds, macular wrinkling, and sometimes associated optic disc edema.

title: Idiopathic Intracranial Hypertension (IIH)
description: IIH is a syndrome of raised intracranial pressure without another structural cause. On OCT it typically shows papilledema with diffuse RNFL thickening, optic nerve head elevation, and sometimes peripapillary subretinal hyporeflective space.

title: Imbruvica-Induced Cystoid Macular Edema
description: Ibrutinib associated cystoid macular edema is a drug-related retinal toxicity reported in patients on Imbruvica therapy. On OCT it is characterised by intraretinal cystic spaces and increased macular thickness without another clear cause.

title: Inner Nuclear Layer Microcysts
description: Inner nuclear layer microcysts are small cystic spaces within the inner nuclear layer that can occur in optic neuropathies and inflammatory disease. On OCT they appear as discrete hyporeflective cavities confined to the INL and may accompany ganglion cell loss.

title: Intra-retinal hyper-reflective foci
description: Intraretinal hyper-reflective foci are punctate bright lesions within the retina seen in exudative and degenerative maculopathies. On OCT they appear as small discrete hyper-reflective dots, often associated with edema, inflammation, or migrating RPE cells.

title: Juxtapapillary CNVM Mimicking Melanocytoma
description: Juxtapapillary CNVM can clinically mimic a pigmented optic nerve lesion such as melanocytoma. On OCT it is characterised by peripapillary subretinal hyper-reflective material with fluid and adjacent outer retinal distortion.

title: Lamellar Hole
description: Lamellar macular hole is a partial-thickness defect of the fovea, often associated with traction or epiretinal proliferation. On OCT it is characterised by an irregular foveal contour with partial tissue loss while the outer retina remains partly intact.

title: Leber Hereditary Optic Neuropathy (LHON)
description: LHON is a mitochondrial optic neuropathy that causes painless central vision loss, usually in young adults. OCT may show acute RNFL thickening early, followed by marked temporal RNFL and ganglion cell loss.

title: Macula-Off Retinal Detachment
description: Macula-off retinal detachment involves separation of the neurosensory retina from the RPE with foveal involvement. On OCT it is characterised by subretinal fluid beneath the macula and detachment of the full-thickness neurosensory retina.

title: Macula-Threatening Retinal Detachment
description: Macula-threatening retinal detachment is a rhegmatogenous detachment approaching but not yet involving the fovea. On OCT it is characterised by subretinal fluid encroaching on the macula with foveal attachment still preserved.

title: Macular Pseudohole
description: Macular pseudohole is a steepened foveal contour caused by centripetal epiretinal membrane contraction rather than a true tissue defect. On OCT it is characterised by a narrow, deep foveal pit with intact outer retinal layers.

title: Macular Telangiectasia
description: Macular telangiectasia refers to perifoveal capillary abnormalities that can produce characteristic structural macular change. On OCT it often shows parafoveal cavitations, temporal foveal thinning, and disruption of the ellipsoid zone.

title: Macular Telangiectasia (Mac Tel) (II)
description: MacTel type 2 is a bilateral perifoveal neurodegenerative and vascular disorder. On OCT it is characterised by temporal or parafoveal cavitations, ellipsoid zone loss, and inner and outer retinal thinning without substantial edema.

title: Melanocytoma
description: Optic disc melanocytoma is a deeply pigmented benign melanocytic tumour of the optic nerve head. On OCT it appears as an elevated lesion with intense posterior shadowing and adjacent retinal or RNFL distortion.

title: Morning Glory Syndrome
description: Morning glory syndrome is a congenital optic disc anomaly with funnel-shaped excavation and abnormal peripapillary tissue. On OCT it is characterised by a deeply excavated disc configuration and may show associated peripapillary or macular detachment.

title: Motion Artefact
description: Motion artefact occurs when the eye moves during acquisition and creates false structural abnormalities on OCT. It may produce abrupt vessel displacement, duplicated contours, or spurious focal RNFL thinning that mimics disease.

title: Multiple Evanescent White Dot Syndrome (MEWDS)
description: MEWDS is an acute unilateral white-dot syndrome that often follows a viral prodrome in young myopic women. On OCT it is characterised by focal disruption of the ellipsoid and interdigitation zones, sometimes with subtle outer retinal hyper-reflectivity.

title: Multiple Myeloma
description: Ocular involvement from multiple myeloma can present with hyperviscosity related retinal or choroidal change. On OCT it may show subretinal fluid, serous macular detachment, or other secondary exudative alterations.

title: Myopic Choroidal Neovascular Membrane
description: Myopic CNVM is neovascularization arising in pathologic myopia from breaks in Bruch membrane and choroidal thinning. On OCT it is characterised by a small subretinal hyper-reflective lesion with overlying fluid or hemorrhage and surrounding myopic change.

title: Neuroretinitis
description: Neuroretinitis is an inflammatory optic neuropathy characterized clinically by disc edema followed by a macular star. On OCT it may show RNFL thickening at the disc with associated serous retinal detachment or intraretinal fluid in the macula.

title: Neuroretinitis (Cat Scratch)
description: Cat scratch neuroretinitis is an infectious optic neuropathy classically associated with Bartonella henselae. On OCT it may show optic disc edema and later macular star associated intraretinal or subretinal fluid with outer retinal change.

title: Non-arteritic ischemic optic neuropathy (NAION)
description: NAION is an acute ischemic optic neuropathy usually occurring in a crowded disc. OCT may show acute sectoral RNFL swelling, followed by chronic RNFL and ganglion cell thinning after the edema resolves.

title: Occlusive Retinal Vasculitits
description: Occlusive retinal vasculitis is an inflammatory vascular disorder that can cause ischemia, hemorrhage, and vision loss. On OCT it may show inner retinal edema or atrophy in ischemic areas, with secondary cystoid macular edema or subretinal fluid.

title: Ocular Toxoplasmosis
description: Ocular toxoplasmosis is a necrotizing retinochoroiditis caused by Toxoplasma gondii. On OCT it may show full-thickness retinal hyper-reflective inflammation, vitritis, and later focal retinal thinning or scar-related disorganization.

title: Optic Disc Drusen
description: Optic disc drusen are calcified hyaline deposits within the optic nerve head that can cause pseudopapilledema. On OCT they appear as hyporeflective cores with hyper-reflective margins, often producing a lumpy bumpy disc contour.

title: Optic Disc Hemorrhage
description: Optic disc hemorrhage is a splinter or flame-shaped hemorrhage at the disc margin and is an important marker of glaucomatous progression. OCT often shows adjacent focal RNFL loss or progressive thinning in the corresponding sector.

title: Optic Nerve Hypoplasia
description: Optic nerve hypoplasia is a congenital underdevelopment of the optic nerve. On OCT it is characterised by a small disc with thinning of the RNFL and ganglion cell layers, often with a double ring appearance clinically.

title: Optic Pit Maculopathy
description: Optic pit maculopathy occurs when fluid tracks from a congenital optic pit into the macula. On OCT it is characterised by schisis-like intraretinal cavities, serous macular detachment, and communication with the optic disc anomaly.

title: Optic Tract Lesion
description: Optic tract lesions cause contralateral homonymous visual field loss and retrograde retinal ganglion cell degeneration. On OCT they are characterised by homonymous hemimacular ganglion cell thinning and corresponding band or bow-tie RNFL loss.

title: Outer Retinal Folds
description: Outer retinal folds are undulations of the photoreceptor and RPE complex that can follow retinal detachment or other mechanical distortion. On OCT they appear as corrugations confined predominantly to the outer retinal layers.

title: Outer Retinal Tubulations (ORT)
description: Outer retinal tubulations are degenerative rearrangements of photoreceptors seen in chronic outer retinal disease. On OCT they appear as round or ovoid hyporeflective spaces with hyper-reflective borders within the outer retina.

title: Pachychoroid Neovasculopathy
description: Pachychoroid neovasculopathy is a type 1 CNV arising in the pachychoroid spectrum. On OCT it is characterised by pachyvessels, a double-layer sign, sub-RPE neovascular tissue, and associated subretinal or intraretinal fluid.

title: Pachychoroid Pigment Epitheliopathy (PPE)
description: PPE is a pachychoroid-spectrum disorder characterized by RPE change without frank serous detachment. On OCT it is characterised by pachyvessels with focal RPE irregularity, shallow PEDs, or a double-layer sign but little to no subretinal fluid.

title: Papilledema
description: Papilledema is optic disc edema secondary to raised intracranial pressure. On OCT it is characterised by diffuse RNFL thickening, optic nerve head elevation, and often a smooth peripapillary subretinal hyporeflective space.

title: Paracentral Acute Middle Maculopathy (PAMM)
description: Paracentral acute middle maculopathy presents with paracentral scotomas from ischemia of the middle retina. On OCT it appears as a placoid hyper-reflective band at the level of the inner nuclear layer and outer plexiform layer, with subsequent inner nuclear layer atrophy.

title: Paracentral Acute Middle Maculopathy (PAMM) (2)
description: PAMM is an ischemic lesion of the intermediate and deep retinal capillary plexuses. On OCT it is characterised acutely by a band-like hyper-reflective lesion in the inner nuclear layer, followed by INL thinning.

title: Partial Central Retinal Artery Occlusion
description: Partial CRAO is an incomplete retinal arterial occlusion with milder or sectoral ischemia than classic CRAO. On OCT it shows inner retinal hyper-reflectivity and swelling in the affected retina, with later atrophy if ischemia persists.

title: Pentosan (Elmiron) Toxicity
description: Pentosan polysulfate or Elmiron toxicity is a pigmentary maculopathy associated with long-term drug exposure. On OCT it is characterised by irregular RPE thickening or excrescences, outer retinal attenuation, and later patchy RPE and ellipsoid zone loss.

title: Peripapillary Atrophy (PPA)
description: Peripapillary atrophy is thinning and degeneration of the retina and RPE around the optic nerve head. On OCT it is characterised by loss of outer retinal and RPE tissue adjacent to the disc, sometimes with exposure of underlying scleral tissue.

title: Peripapillary Choroidal Neovascular Membrane
description: Peripapillary CNVM is neovascular growth adjacent to the optic nerve head. On OCT it is characterised by peripapillary subretinal hyper-reflective material with adjacent fluid, hemorrhage, or pigment epithelial detachment.

title: Peripapillary Pachychoroid Syndrome
description: Peripapillary pachychoroid syndrome is a pachychoroid-spectrum disorder centred around the optic nerve and nasal macula. On OCT it is characterised by peripapillary choroidal thickening with overlying intraretinal or subretinal fluid and RPE irregularity.

title: Peripheral Exudative Hemorrhagic Chorioretinopathy (PEHCR)
description: PEHCR is a peripheral degenerative disorder marked by exudation and hemorrhage that can mimic choroidal melanoma. On OCT it may show peripheral pigment epithelial detachment, subretinal blood, and exudative retinal detachment.

title: Pituitary Apoplexy
description: Pituitary apoplexy is acute hemorrhage or infarction within a pituitary adenoma that can rapidly compress the optic chiasm. OCT may later show binasal ganglion cell loss or RNFL thinning from chiasmal injury.

title: Polypoidal Choroidal Vasculopathy (PCV)
description: PCV is an aneurysmal type of neovascular disease within the pachychoroid or AMD spectrum. On OCT it is characterised by sharp peaked or notched PEDs, subretinal fluid, and a thick choroid with pachyvessels.

title: Poppers Maculopathy
description: Poppers maculopathy is a toxic foveopathy linked to inhaled alkyl nitrites. On OCT it is characterised by focal disruption of the subfoveal ellipsoid and interdigitation zones.

title: Post-Retinal Detachment Outcome
description: After retinal detachment repair, persistent visual limitation often relates to residual outer retinal damage rather than recurrent detachment. On OCT this may appear as persistent subretinal fluid, outer retinal folds, or ellipsoid zone disruption.

title: Posterior Placoid Chorioretinitis
description: Posterior placoid chorioretinitis describes placoid inflammatory lesions at the posterior pole, classically seen in syphilitic disease. On OCT it is characterised by outer retinal and ellipsoid zone disruption with granular RPE change and placoid hyper-reflective material.

title: Posterior Scleritis
description: Posterior scleritis is a painful inflammatory disorder of the posterior sclera. OCT may show choroidal thickening, serous retinal detachment, choroidal folds, or cystoid macular edema secondary to inflammation.

title: Posterior Staphyloma
description: Posterior staphyloma is an outpouching of the posterior scleral wall typically seen in pathologic myopia. On OCT it is characterised by abrupt posterior bowing of the eye wall with associated myopic retinal and choroidal thinning.

title: Pre-Choroidal Cleft
description: Pre-choroidal cleft is a hyporeflective space between Bruch membrane and hyper-reflective fibrovascular material within a PED, usually in neovascular AMD. Its presence has been associated with type 3 neovascularization and increased risk of RPE tear.

title: Pre-Perimetric Glaucoma
description: Pre-perimetric glaucoma is structural glaucomatous optic neuropathy before repeatable visual field loss becomes apparent. On OCT it is characterised by focal RNFL and ganglion cell thinning, often in the inferior or superior vulnerability zones.

title: Previous Retinal Vein Occlusion (RVO)
description: A previous retinal vein occlusion may leave chronic ischemic or atrophic retinal change after the edema resolves. On OCT it is characterised by sectoral inner retinal thinning, disorganization, or residual cystic change in the prior affected territory.

title: Punctate Inner Choroidopathy (PIC)
description: PIC is an inflammatory chorioretinopathy that typically affects young myopic women. On OCT it is characterised by focal RPE elevation or interruption with underlying choroidal involvement, and active lesions may show subretinal fluid or CNVM.

title: Purtscher's Retinopathy
description: Purtscher retinopathy is a traumatic or complement mediated occlusive microangiopathy. On OCT it may show inner retinal hyper-reflectivity and thickening from ischemia, sometimes with macular edema or serous detachment.

title: Radiation Retinopathy
description: Radiation retinopathy is a progressive occlusive microangiopathy after ocular or periocular radiotherapy. On OCT it is characterised by macular edema, intraretinal cysts, ischemic thinning, and later atrophic retinal change.

title: RAP Lesion (Type 3 CNVM)
description: RAP, or type 3 neovascularization, begins within the retina and progresses toward the RPE and choroid. On OCT it is characterised by intraretinal hyper-reflective foci or a focal neovascular complex with cystoid edema, PED, and surrounding fluid.

title: Rathke's Cleft Cyst
description: Rathke cleft cyst is a benign sellar lesion that can compress the optic chiasm when enlarged. OCT may reveal binasal ganglion cell complex thinning and corresponding RNFL loss from chiasmal compression.

title: Red Disease (Split Bundle)
description: Red disease describes a false positive OCT abnormality caused by an anatomic variant or technical limitation rather than true glaucoma. In split bundle red disease, a normal variant RNFL bundle pattern is colour coded as abnormally thin despite the absence of matching structural or functional loss.

title: Resolving Central Serous Chorioretinopathy (CSR)
description: Resolving CSCR is the recovery phase after serous retinal detachment begins to subside. On OCT it is characterised by decreasing subretinal fluid with residual outer retinal irregularity or small PEDs that may persist temporarily.

title: Reticular Pseudodrusen
description: Reticular pseudodrusen, or subretinal drusenoid deposits, are located above the RPE rather than below it. On OCT they appear as small mound-like deposits in the subretinal space, often associated with outer retinal thinning.

title: Retinal Metastases
description: Retinal metastases are rare intraocular metastatic deposits involving the neurosensory retina. On OCT they may appear as hyper-reflective intraretinal masses with retinal disorganization, shadowing, and associated fluid or exudation.

title: Retinoblastoma (RB)
description: Retinoblastoma is the most common primary intraocular malignancy of childhood. On OCT it can show a hyper-reflective retinal mass with calcification, posterior shadowing, and overlying subretinal fluid or retinal detachment.

title: Retinoschisis
description: Retinoschisis is splitting of the neurosensory retina into separate layers. On OCT it is characterised by smooth walled intraretinal cavities with vertical bridging columns, distinguishing it from cystoid edema.

title: Retrograde Trans-Synaptic Degeneration
description: Retrograde trans-synaptic degeneration is ganglion cell loss that occurs after injury to the post-geniculate visual pathway. On OCT it is characterised by homonymous hemimacular ganglion cell thinning that respects the vertical meridian.

title: Sclerochoroidal Calcification
description: Sclerochoroidal calcification is a benign calcified scleral plaque that can indent the choroid. On OCT it appears as an elevated choroidal-scleral lesion with posterior shadowing and compression of the overlying choroid.

title: Serpiginous Chorioretinopathy
description: Serpiginous chorioretinopathy is a recurrent inflammatory choroidopathy with geographic spread from the peripapillary region. On OCT it is characterised by outer retinal and RPE disruption, thinning, and active edge hyper-reflectivity.

title: Severe Glaucoma
description: Severe glaucoma is advanced glaucomatous optic neuropathy with marked ganglion cell and RNFL loss. On OCT it is characterised by diffuse or near-floor thinning of the RNFL and ganglion cell complex with corresponding optic nerve cupping.

title: Sickle Cell Maculopathy
description: Sickle cell maculopathy is ischemic retinal damage related to sickling in the deep capillary plexus. On OCT it is characterised by temporal or parafoveal inner retinal thinning and may overlap with PAMM-like change.

title: Solar Retinopathy
description: Solar retinopathy is photic injury to the fovea after sun gazing or eclipse viewing. On OCT it is characterised by focal disruption of the foveal outer retina, especially the ellipsoid and interdigitation zones.

title: Stargardt Disease
description: Stargardt disease is an inherited macular dystrophy caused most commonly by ABCA4 mutations. On OCT it is characterised by loss of the ellipsoid zone, thinning of the outer retina, and progressive RPE atrophy in the macula.

title: Stellate Nonhereditary Idiopathic Foveomacular Retinoschisis (SNIFR)
description: SNIFR is a nonhereditary macular schisis usually centred on the fovea. On OCT it is characterised by spoke-like splitting of the outer plexiform or Henle fibre layer with a stellate cystic pattern and preserved outer retina.

title: Sub-Inner Limiting Membrane Hemorrhage
description: Sub-ILM hemorrhage is a premacular hemorrhage located beneath the inner limiting membrane. On OCT it is characterised by a dense hyper-reflective collection under the ILM with marked posterior shadowing.

title: Sub-Retinal Fluid
description: Subretinal fluid is accumulation of fluid between the neurosensory retina and the RPE. On OCT it appears as a hyporeflective space elevating the neurosensory retina off the RPE.

title: Subretinal hyper-reflective material (SHRM)
description: SHRM is hyper-reflective material in the subretinal space often associated with neovascular AMD. On OCT it appears as a plaque-like subretinal hyper-reflective lesion and frequently coexists with fluid or fibrovascular PED.

title: Sympathetic Ophthalmia
description: Sympathetic ophthalmia is a bilateral granulomatous uveitis that follows penetrating ocular trauma or surgery. On OCT it may show multifocal serous retinal detachments or bacillary detachment with associated choroidal thickening.

title: Syphilitic Outer Retinopathy
description: Syphilitic outer retinopathy is an ocular syphilis phenotype that can mimic AZOOR. On OCT it is characterised by ellipsoid zone disruption and focal RPE excrescences, often matching hyperautofluorescent areas.

title: Torpedo Maculopathy
description: Torpedo maculopathy is a congenital, usually unilateral lesion temporal to the fovea. On OCT it is characterised by attenuation of the outer retina and RPE, sometimes with a subretinal cleft or focal choroidal excavation.

title: Valsalva Retinopathy
description: Valsalva retinopathy is a premacular hemorrhage caused by sudden rises in intrathoracic or intra-abdominal pressure. On OCT it is characterised by a hyper-reflective sub-ILM or subhyaloid hemorrhage with dense posterior shadowing.

title: Vitreomacular Traction (VMT)
description: VMT is abnormal persistent adhesion of the posterior hyaloid to the macula that exerts tractional force. On OCT it is characterised by incomplete posterior vitreous detachment with distortion or elevation of the fovea and sometimes cystic change.

title: Vitreopapillary Traction (VPT)
description: Vitreopapillary traction is persistent adherence of the posterior hyaloid to the optic nerve head. On OCT it is characterised by focal vitreous attachment elevating the disc and simulating optic disc edema.

title: Vitreopapillary Traction (VPT; III)
description: Vitreopapillary traction is persistent adherence of the posterior hyaloid to the optic nerve head. On OCT it is characterised by focal vitreous attachment elevating the disc and simulating optic disc edema.

title: Vitreous Hemorrhage
description: Vitreous hemorrhage appears on OCT as diffuse or punctate hyper-reflective opacities within the vitreous cavity. Dense hemorrhage can obscure deeper retinal detail and may be accompanied by posterior shadowing.

title: Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada Disease (VKH)
description: VKH is a multisystem autoimmune disorder that causes bilateral granulomatous panuveitis. On OCT it is characterised by choroidal thickening with multiple serous retinal detachments, septae, and sometimes bacillary detachment.

title: Von Hippel-Lindau (VHL) disease
description: VHL disease is a hereditary tumour syndrome associated with retinal hemangioblastomas. On OCT these lesions appear as full-thickness hyper-reflective retinal masses with adjacent exudation, cysts, or subretinal fluid.

title: Wyburn-Mason Syndrome
description: Wyburn-Mason syndrome is a congenital arteriovenous malformation syndrome affecting the retina and brain. On OCT it is characterised by large hyper-reflective inner retinal vascular channels with posterior shadowing and may have adjacent intraretinal fluid.

title: X-Linked Retinoschisis
description: X-linked retinoschisis is an inherited retinal dystrophy causing foveal splitting in boys and young men. On OCT it is characterised by schitic cavities with thin vertical bridging columns, most prominently in the fovea.
